mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes, facilitating migration and disaster recovery. It is particularly useful in environments where data loss can have significant consequences, but it requires careful management of backup storage and restore processes.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) necessary for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-v1.18.0-crds.yaml

```

steps.title: Install Velero
steps.description:
This step installs the Velero server using Helm with the specified version and namespace.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

```
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Velero pods are running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

steps.title: Create a backup
steps.description:
This step creates a backup of the current cluster resources using Velero.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces my-namespace

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups and data associated with Velero.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current state. This step will upgrade Velero to the latest version.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces my-namespace

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Create a backup before upgrading.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified backup storage location is not accessible or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your backup storage configuration and ensure that the bucket exists and is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Velero server is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Velero pod has crashed or failed to start.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl logs deployment/velero -n velero' to check the logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that there are no resources in the specified namespace to back up.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the namespace contains resources and that you are specifying the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Volume snapshot failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the volume snapshot location is not configured correctly or if permissions are missing.

**Fix:**
Verify the volume snapshot location configuration and ensure that the necessary permissions are granted.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional cloud providers, improved backup performance, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17 or earlier, ensure that your backup storage location is compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
